textPlain: As a Sindhi speaker myself, amazing stuff. The output is so good. This unlocks the vastness of the internet for millions of people. I am imaging something like NotebookLM but for under-served languages or a hotline where people can call and talk/learn about anything. Do you guys have plans to create b2c products yourself?
Nice! Clearly a big and underserved market for voice AI solutions.Would be nice to have some code examples for using your TTS API with Pipecat.

Nice, this is really needed. Would be cool to see some of the less common regional Chinese dialects, which are widely spoken and often the only language older people speak. And even just more accurate regional accents for Mandarin.
Congrats on the launch! Having support for regional voices is going to open up so many opportunities.
Pretty cool! Do you think the model would be good at other under-served languages as well? Or is it hypertuned to just these?
Would love to see Malayalam here one day!

At the moment we are focused on making the models available through API so developers can make some cool things. We are actively monitoring to see if there is an opportunity that we will be better positioned to solve.We are planning on hosting an online hackathon soon, so will suggest these things as ideas!
I have to make that.. I did make one for LiveKit which utilizes our websocket API designed for real-time conversation API:https://docs.upliftai.org/tutorials/livekit-voice-agent

btw I did try to first make it with Pipecat and was having some annoying windows issues with getting libraries installed for daily etc. so I posted something that was easily reproducible for the tutorial...
wow did not know that! Do you feel there is gap in speech understanding here or personalization missing with current TTS?

The model itself can work well for new languages, its just the process of data gathering and maintaining high quality of data is what we have to figure out as we scale across languages.Currently the model is only given data for these languages so it doesn't know anything else.
Yes! I will keep track of this comment for the day we do :P
